nativeRange.habitat: Shallow reef and rocky subtidal zones in the photic zone

decorPreferences: zip-tie or fishing line to rock

avoidWith: heavy herbivores (tangs, rabbitfish) in small tanks

imageCandidates.notes: Product image from Mosaic Macros Red Sword listing. Shows reddish-brown serrated sword-form blades.
Product image from Mosaic Macros Corkscrew Saw listing — corkscrew-twisted blade morph of the same genus.

summary: The Red Sword is an ornamental red macroalga prized for its elongated, reddish-brown blades with finely serrated edges. It is also sold under the name Corkscrew Saw in a twisted-blade form. In the aquarium, it is kept attached to rockwork as a display piece rather than for nutrient export.
careNotes:
The Red Sword (Neurymenia sp.) is a specialty ornamental red macroalga in the family Rhodomelaceae, producing elongated, reddish-brown blades with serrated margins. Two blade forms are commonly sold under this genus: straight sword blades, traded as Red Sword, and corkscrew-twisted blades, traded as Corkscrew Saw. Both are identified to genus only and should not be confused with [red-sawblade], which covers Neurymenia fraxinifolia — the species-level form traded under that name.

Water parameters generally advised for this species follow standard reef targets: temperature 22–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, alkalinity 8–12 dKH, specific gravity 1.023–1.026. Calcium 400–450 ppm and magnesium 1250–1350 ppm are recommended to support overall reef health. Nitrate 5–20 ppm and phosphate 0.02–0.1 ppm are considered acceptable ranges.

SAND BED MELT WARNING: As a red alga (Rhodophyta), Neurymenia sp. must NOT be placed in or buried in sand. Red and brown macroalgae are widely reported to melt at the base when their tissue contacts sand for extended periods. This species must be attached to hard substrate — preferred methods are wedging between rockwork (least stress), zip-tie or fishing line loosely secured to rock, or careful placement on live rock. Superglue is generally discouraged for long-term attachment.

Lighting should be full-spectrum at medium intensity; actinic-only lighting cannot support this or similar specialty macroalgae. Flow equivalent to a soft-coral reef tank is recommended — enough for water exchange without mechanically damaging the delicate blade edges. Weekly dosing of iron, iodine, and other trace micronutrients is generally advised for macroalgae of this type. Growth rate is medium and nutrient uptake is low; the Red Sword is kept primarily for ornamental display rather than nutrient export.
breedingNotes: Propagation in captivity is generally accomplished by blade fragmentation — trimming healthy blade sections and securing them to rockwork by wedging or with zip-tie or fishing line. Sand-bed propagation is not appropriate for red algae. Sexual reproduction, which in red algae involves an alternating lifecycle between spore-producing and gamete-producing generations, has not been documented in hobby aquaria for this genus.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Primary product listing for Red Sword (Neurymenia sp.). Confirms serrated reddish-brown sword blades, notes common misidentification as Cryptonemia crennulata. No specific water-parameter data on product page — parameters sourced from Mosaic general care guide.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Macroalgae Care: The Basics
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source providing authoritative parameter targets (temp 22–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, Ca 400–450 ppm, Mg 1250–1350 ppm, NO3 5–20 ppm, PO4 0.02–0.1 ppm). Confirms red algae sand-bed melt rule and rock-attachment propagation guidance. Also accessed: Corkscrew Saw variant page (https://mosaicmacros.com/products/corkscrew-saw-macroalgae-neurymenia-sp) — identical genus description, consolidated as alsoKnownAs.
